name: Spring Rolls
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine pork, 1 tablespoon soy sauce, sherry, and garlic salt in a bowl. Let stand for 15 minutes.
2. Heat 2 tablespoons oil in a hot wok or large skillet over medium-high heat. Brown the pork mixture in the hot oil.
3. Add bean sprouts and onion to the pan. Stir-fry until vegetables are tender-crisp. Remove from heat and let cool.
4. Dissolve cornstarch in 1/4 cup water.
5. Place about 1/3 cup of pork mixture on the lower half of an egg roll skin.
6. Moisten the left and right edges with the cornstarch mixture.
7. Fold the bottom edge up to just cover the filling.
8. Fold the left and right edges halfway over; roll into a jelly-roll shape.
9. Moisten the top edge with the cornstarch mixture and seal.
10. Repeat with all rolls.
11. Combine biscuit mix, egg, and remaining 1/4 cup water in a small bowl. Dip each roll in the batter.
12. Heat oil for frying in a hot wok or large skillet over medium-high heat to 370°F (180°C).
13. Deep fry the rolls, a few at a time, in the hot oil for 5 to 7 minutes, or until golden brown. Turn often.
14. Drain on paper towels.
15. Slice each roll into 4 pieces. Serve with hot mustard, catsup, and soy sauce as desired.
